skill.name: Azure.ResourceManager.DurableTask (.NET)
skill.description: Azure Resource Manager SDK for Durable Task Scheduler management in .NET
skill.summary: Management plane SDK for provisioning and managing Azure Durable Task Scheduler resources via Azure Resource Manager
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides reference documentation for Azure Resource Manager SDK usage for Durable Task Scheduler management. No prompt injection attempts or malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Provision Azure Durable Task Schedulers with .NET
content.value_statement: Manage Azure Durable Task Scheduler resources efficiently using the Azure Resource Manager SDK. This skill guides you through creating schedulers, task hubs, and retention policies with proper authentication and best practices.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage Durable Task Scheduler resources in Azure
Configure task hubs under schedulers for orchestration workflows
Set up retention policies for orchestration history
Manage SKU selection between Dedicated and Consumption options
Handle IP allowlists for network security configuration
Perform CRUD operations on Azure Resource Manager Durable Task resources
limitations: Management plane only - does not start orchestrations or query instances
Requires Azure subscription and appropriate RBAC permissions
Data plane operations require separate Microsoft.DurableTask.Client.AzureManaged package
Long-running operations require proper WaitUntil handling

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Provisioning Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Automate the creation of Durable Task Scheduler resources as part of infrastructure deployment pipelines

use_cases.title: Application Developer Setting Up Orchestrations
use_cases.description: Provision the required Azure resources before deploying orchestration code to production

use_cases.title: Cloud Architect Designing Solutions
use_cases.description: Evaluate and configure Durable Task Scheduler options for enterprise workflow solutions

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Create a basic scheduler
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Durable Task Scheduler in my Azure subscription using the Azure.ResourceManager.DurableTask SDK. I have my subscription ID and resource group ready.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time setup of Durable Task infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: List existing schedulers
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to list all Durable Task Schedulers in my Azure subscription and display their properties.
prompt_templates.scenario: Inventory and discovery of existing resources

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Configure scheduler with IP restrictions
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to create a Durable Task Scheduler with IP allowlist restrictions for production security. Show me the complete code with proper error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production deployment with network security requirements

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Full lifecycle management
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete example that provisions a Durable Task Scheduler with Dedicated SKU, creates a task hub, configures retention policy, and includes cleanup code for resource deletion.

output_examples.input: Create a Durable Task Scheduler named 'production-scheduler' in East US with Dedicated SKU
output_examples.output: Successfully created Durable Task Scheduler 'production-scheduler' in East US with Dedicated SKU (1 instance). Endpoint: https://production-scheduler.eastus.durabletask.azure.com/

output_examples.input: List all schedulers in the subscription and show their status
output_examples.output: Found 3 schedulers: production-scheduler (Dedicated, Running), dev-scheduler (Consumption, Running), test-scheduler (Dedicated, Stopped)

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication to support multiple credential types including managed identity
Always use WaitUntil.Completed for operations that must finish before dependent actions proceed
Delete task hubs before deleting their parent schedulers to avoid resource conflicts
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode credentials or connection strings in source code
Avoid using WaitUntil.Started without implementing proper polling logic
Do not skip error handling for RequestFailedException when calling ARM APIs

faq.question: What is the difference between management plane and data plane for Durable Task?
faq.answer: Management plane (this SDK) handles resource provisioning like creating schedulers and task hubs. Data plane handles runtime operations like starting orchestrations and querying instances.

faq.question: Which SKU should I choose for my Durable Task Scheduler?
faq.answer: Use Dedicated SKU for production workloads requiring predictable performance. Use Consumption SKU for development or variable workloads that benefit from serverless auto-scaling.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Resource Manager?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically tries multiple authentication methods including managed identity, Azure CLI credentials, and environment variables.

faq.question: Can I update a scheduler after creation?
faq.answer: Yes, use CreateOrUpdateAsync to modify scheduler properties like SKU capacity or IP allowlist. The operation is idempotent and safe to call multiple times.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a scheduler that has task hubs?
faq.answer: You must delete all task hubs under a scheduler before deleting the scheduler itself. Attempting to delete a scheduler with existing task hubs will fail.

faq.question: How do I handle long-running operations in the SDK?
faq.answer: Use WaitUntil.Completed to block until the operation finishes, or WaitUntil.Started to begin the operation and poll for completion manually using the returned operation object.
